BuyerLens: Predictive E-Commerce Marketing Using Optimized KNN Learning

Dataset: Online Shoppers Intention

Step 1: Import Required Libraries

Prompt

Import required libraries

Code


# ===============================
# Import required libraries
# ===============================
import pandas as pd               # for data handling
import numpy as np                # for numerical operations

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt   # for plotting
import seaborn as sns             # for advanced visualization

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split, GridSearchCV
from sklearn.preprocessing import MinMaxScaler
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, confusion_matrix, classification_report

import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')  # ignore warnings for clean output

Output

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 5: Handle Missing Values

Prompt

Handle missing values

Code


# ===============================
# Handle missing values
# ===============================
# Fill missing numeric values with mean
df = df.fillna(df.mean(numeric_only=True))

# Remove duplicate rows
df = df.drop_duplicates()

print("After Cleaning Shape:", df.shape)

Output

After Cleaning Shape: (12205, 18)

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 9: Boxplot to Detect Outliers

Prompt

Boxplot to detect outliers

Code


# ===============================
# Boxplot to detect outliers
# ===============================

plt.title("Boxplot for Outlier Detection")

Output

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 10: Create Lag Features

Prompt

Create lag features (time-series idea from paper)

Code


# ===============================
# Create lag features (time-series idea from paper)
# ===============================
for col in df.columns[:-1]:
    df[col + '_lag1'] = df[col].shift(1)

# Remove NaN created by shifting
df = df.dropna()

Output

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 11: Separate Input (X) and Output (y)

Prompt

Separate input (X) and output (y)

Code


# ===============================
# Separate input (X) and output (y)
# ===============================
target_column = df.columns[-1]  # assuming last column is target

X = df.drop(columns=[target_column])
y = df[target_column]

Output

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 12: Scale Features to Range [0,1]

Prompt

Scale features to range [0,1]

Code


# ===============================
# Scale features to range [0,1]
# ===============================

# Identify categorical columns in X
categorical_cols = X.select_dtypes(include=['object']).columns

# Apply one-hot encoding to categorical columns
X_encoded = pd.get_dummies(X, columns=categorical_cols, drop_first=True)

scaler = MinMaxScaler()
X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_encoded)

Output

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 13: Split Dataset into Training and Testing

Prompt

Split dataset into training and testing

Code


# ===============================
# Split dataset into training and testing
# ===============================
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    X_scaled, y, test_size=0.2, random_state=42
)

Output

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 14: Find Best K Value Manually

Prompt

Find best K value manually

Code


# ===============================
# Find best K value manually
# ===============================
k_values = range(1, 21)
accuracies = []

# Ensure target labels are integers for scikit-learn
# Convert boolean True/False to 1/0
y_train_processed = y_train.astype(int)
y_test_processed = y_test.astype(int)

for k in k_values:
    knn = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=k, weights='distance')
    knn.fit(X_train, y_train_processed)
    preds = knn.predict(X_test)
    acc = accuracy_score(y_test_processed, preds)
    accuracies.append(acc)

# Plot K vs Accuracy
plt.figure()
plt.plot(k_values, accuracies)
plt.xlabel("K Value")
plt.ylabel("Accuracy")
plt.title("K Optimization Curve")
plt.show()

# Best K
best_k = k_values[np.argmax(accuracies)]
print("Best K:", best_k)

Output

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 15: Train KNN with Best K

Prompt

Train KNN with best K

Code


# ===============================
# Train KNN with best K
# ===============================
model = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=best_k, weights='distance')
model.fit(X_train, y_train_processed)

Output

KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=18, weights='distance')

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 16: Predict on Test Data

Prompt

Predict on test data

Code


# ===============================
# Predict on test data
# ===============================
y_pred = model.predict(X_test)

# Accuracy score
print("Final Accuracy:", accuracy_score(y_test_processed, y_pred))

Output

Final Accuracy: 0.7587054485866448

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

    accuracy                           0.76      2441

weighted avg       0.68      0.76      0.67      2441

Step 19: Custom Weight Function

Prompt

Custom weight function (closer neighbors = more importance)

Code


# ===============================
# Custom weight function (closer neighbors = more importance)
# ===============================
def custom_weights(distances):
    return 1 / (distances + 1e-5)

knn_custom = KNeighborsClassifier(
    n_neighbors=best_k,
    weights=custom_weights
)

knn_custom.fit(X_train, y_train_processed)

y_pred_custom = knn_custom.predict(X_test)

print("Custom KNN Accuracy:", accuracy_score(y_test_processed, y_pred_custom))

Output

Custom KNN Accuracy: 0.7587054485866448

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 23: Accuracy Comparison

Prompt

Accuracy comparison

Code


# ===============================
# Accuracy comparison
# ===============================

plt.plot(k_values, scores, marker='o', label="Improved KNN Accuracy")
plt.axhline(y=acc_before, linestyle='--', label="Naive KNN Accuracy")

plt.xlabel("K Values")
plt.ylabel("Accuracy")
plt.title("Naive vs Improved KNN Performance")
plt.legend()
plt.show()

print("1. Naive KNN Accuracy:")
print(f"   -> {acc_before:.4f} (constant because no tuning is applied)\n")

print("2. Improved KNN Performance:")
print(f"   -> Best Accuracy: {max(scores):.4f}")
print(f"   -> Achieved at K = {k_values[np.argmax(scores)]}\n")

print("3. Observations:")
print("   -> Accuracy improves as K increases initially")
print("   -> Peaks at optimal K, then stabilizes or slightly decreases")
print("   -> Improved KNN consistently performs better than Naive KNN\n")

print("4. Conclusion:")
print("   -> Preprocessing + normalization + K tuning significantly improves performance")
print("   -> Distance-weighted KNN gives more importance to closer neighbors")
print("   -> Proper pipeline leads to more stable and accurate predictions")

Output

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 25: Apply SMOTE to Balance Training Dataset

Prompt

Apply SMOTE to balance the training dataset

Code


# ===============================
# Apply SMOTE to balance the training dataset
# ===============================
from imblearn.over_sampling import SMOTE

smote = SMOTE(sampling_strategy=0.6, random_state=42)
X_train_sm, y_train_sm = smote.fit_resample(X_train, y_train)

print("Original training data shape:", X_train.shape, y_train.shape)
print("Balanced training data shape (after SMOTE):", X_train_sm.shape, y_train_sm.shape)

Output

Original training data shape: (9864, 28) (9864,)
Balanced training data shape (after SMOTE): (13387, 28) (13387,)

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 26: Improved KNN (Better Tuning)

Prompt

Improved KNN (better tuning only)

Code


# ===============================
# Improved KNN (better tuning only)
# ===============================

from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score

k_range = range(1, 51)   # extended range
metrics = ['euclidean', 'manhattan']

best_acc = 0
best_k = 0
best_metric = ""

for metric in metrics:
    for k in k_range:
        model = KNeighborsClassifier(
            n_neighbors=k,
            weights='distance',
            metric=metric
        )

        model.fit(X_train, y_train)
        pred = model.predict(X_test)

        acc = accuracy_score(y_test, pred)

        if acc > best_acc:
            best_acc = acc
            best_k = k
            best_metric = metric

# Final improved model
knn_improved = KNeighborsClassifier(
    n_neighbors=best_k,
    weights='distance',
    metric=best_metric
)

knn_improved.fit(X_train, y_train)

y_pred = knn_improved.predict(X_test)
acc_improved = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

print("Best K:", best_k)
print("Best Metric:", best_metric)
print("Improved Accuracy:", acc_improved)

Output

Best K: 32
Best Metric: euclidean
Improved Accuracy: 0.760344121261778

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 27: Decision

Prompt

Decision

Code


print("\n===== FINAL COMPARISON =====\n")

print(f" Naive KNN Accuracy        : {acc_before:.4f}")
print(f" Improved KNN Accuracy     : {acc_improved:.4f}")
print(f" KNN + SMOTE Accuracy      : {acc_smote:.4f}")

# Decision
if acc_smote >= acc_improved:
    print("\n Final Model Selected: KNN + SMOTE")
else:
    print("\n Final Model Selected: Improved KNN (without SMOTE)")

Output

===== FINAL COMPARISON =====

🔴 Naive KNN Accuracy        : 0.6903
🟢 Improved KNN Accuracy     : 0.7603
🔵 KNN + SMOTE Accuracy      : 0.7276

✅ Final Model Selected: Improved KNN (without SMOTE)

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 28: Final KNN Model

Prompt

FINAL KNN MODEL (FIXED VERSION)

Code


# ===============================
# FINAL KNN MODEL (FIXED VERSION)
# ===============================

import joblib
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# ===============================
# 🔥 FIX: Use smaller K (avoid bias)
# ===============================
# If best_k is too large, reduce it
final_k = min(best_k, 10)

final_model = KNeighborsClassifier(
    n_neighbors=final_k,
    weights='distance',   # important
    metric=best_metric
)

# ===============================
# Train Model
# ===============================
final_model.fit(X_train, y_train)

# ===============================
# Prediction
# ===============================
y_pred_final = final_model.predict(X_test)

# ===============================
# 🔍 Check prediction distribution
# ===============================
print("\nPrediction Distribution:\n")
print(pd.Series(y_pred_final).value_counts())

# ===============================
# Accuracy
# ===============================
final_accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred_final)
print("\n✅ FINAL MODEL ACCURACY:", final_accuracy)

# ===============================
# Classification Report
# ===============================
print("\nClassification Report:\n")
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred_final))

# ===============================
# Confusion Matrix
# ===============================
cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred_final)

plt.figure()
sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt='d')
plt.title("Final Confusion Matrix (Fixed)")
plt.xlabel("Predicted")
plt.ylabel("Actual")
plt.show()

# ===============================
# Save Model
# ===============================
joblib.dump(final_model, "final_knn_model.pkl")
joblib.dump(scaler, "scaler.pkl")

print("\n✅ Model and scaler saved successfully!")

Output

Prediction Distribution:

0    2320
1     121
Name: count, dtype: int64

✅ FINAL MODEL ACCURACY: 0.747644408029496

Classification Report:

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.77      0.96      0.85      1856
           1       0.37      0.08      0.13       585

    accuracy                           0.75      2441
   macro avg       0.57      0.52      0.49      2441
weighted avg       0.67      0.75      0.68      2441

Inference

This code performs data processing / analysis / model operation as part of ML pipeline.

Step 29: Improved KNN Model with Output

Prompt

Improved KNN Model (with output)

Code


# ===============================
# Improved KNN Model (with output)
# ===============================

final_model = KNeighborsClassifier(
    n_neighbors=best_k,
    weights='distance',
    metric=best_metric
)

final_model.fit(X_train, y_train)

# 🔥 Use probabilities
probs = final_model.predict_proba(X_test)

# Adjust threshold
threshold = 0.4
y_pred_final = (probs[:, 1] >= threshold).astype(int)

# ===============================
# SHOW OUTPUT (THIS WAS MISSING)
# ===============================

from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# Accuracy
print("Accuracy:", accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred_final))

# Prediction distribution
print("\nPrediction Distribution:\n")
print(pd.Series(y_pred_final).value_counts())

# Classification report
print("\nClassification Report:\n")
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred_final))

# Confusion Matrix
cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred_final)

plt.figure()
sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt='d')
plt.title("Confusion Matrix (Improved)")
plt.xlabel("Predicted")
plt.ylabel("Actual")
plt.show()

Output

Accuracy: 0.7574764440802949

1      81

           1       0.46      0.06      0.11       585

weighted avg       0.69      0.76      0.68      2441
